Operation 'refundPayment' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The description lacks clarity on input requirements (pay_id parameter details), what is returned (Payment object structure), and side effects (transaction state changes). Added these details to help AI agents understand the complete operation flow and determine appropriate usage.

data.current_description: Initiates a refund for a specific payment transaction identified by pay_id.
data.description_suggestion: Initiates a refund for a specific payment transaction identified by pay_id. Requires the payment ID as input. Returns a Payment object reflecting the refunded transaction status. This operation reverses the original payment and updates the transaction state accordingly.
data.reasoning: The description lacks clarity on input requirements (pay_id parameter details), what is returned (Payment object structure), and side effects (transaction state changes). Added these details to help AI agents understand the complete operation flow and determine appropriate usage.

message: Operation 'generatePdf' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Description is too generic and doesn't specify what transaction type is supported, what the PDF contains, or the output format. Added context about transaction types, PDF content, and use cases to improve AI agent interpretability and help determine when this operation should be used.

data.current_description: Generates a PDF document for a specific transaction
data.description_suggestion: Generates a PDF document for a specific transaction. Accepts a transaction identifier as input and returns a PDF file containing transaction details. The PDF includes payment or payout information depending on the transaction type. Useful for record-keeping and sharing transaction documentation.
data.reasoning: Description is too generic and doesn't specify what transaction type is supported, what the PDF contains, or the output format. Added context about transaction types, PDF content, and use cases to improve AI agent interpretability and help determine when this operation should be used.

Description lacks specificity about inputs required, output format, and what information is included in the invoice. Added details about input parameters, return format, and business context (financial reporting, audit trails) to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation effectively.

data.current_description: Generates a tax invoice document for accounting purposes
data.summary_suggestion: Generate tax invoice document for transaction
data.description_suggestion: Generates a tax invoice document for a specific transaction for accounting and compliance purposes. Requires a transaction identifier as input. Returns a tax invoice document containing itemized transaction details, tax calculations, and compliance information. The generated invoice can be used for financial reporting and audit trails.
data.reasoning: Description lacks specificity about inputs required, output format, and what information is included in the invoice. Added details about input parameters, return format, and business context (financial reporting, audit trails) to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation effectively.

Enhanced both to clarify that this operation specifically exports Payout transaction data (not general transactions), what parameters are accepted, CSV column structure, and practical applications for AI agents to determine appropriate usage scenarios.

data.current_description: Generates a CSV file of the Payout transaction details
data.summary_suggestion: Generate CSV export of payout transactions
data.description_suggestion: Generates a CSV file containing detailed payout transaction records. Accepts filtering or selection parameters to specify which payout transactions to include. Returns a CSV-formatted file with columns for transaction ID, amount, status, date, and other payout-specific details. Useful for bulk data export, reconciliation, and external system integration.
data.reasoning: Summary is generic and doesn't specify it's payout-specific. Description lacks input parameter details, output structure, and use cases. Enhanced both to clarify that this operation specifically exports Payout transaction data (not general transactions), what parameters are accepted, CSV column structure, and practical applications for AI agents to determine appropriate usage scenarios.

message: Operation 'createSplitLink' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is adequate, but description lacks clarity on how split payments work and what the callback URL does. Added explanation of fund distribution mechanism and callback purpose to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

data.current_summary: Create a new split payment link
data.current_description:
Generates a new split payment link using the provided recipients, amount, recipient note, and callback URL.
This link can be shared with customers for split payment processing.
data.description_suggestion: Generates a new split payment link that distributes payments among multiple recipients. Accepts recipients list, total amount, recipient note, and optional callback URL for payment notifications. Returns a shareable split payment link that can be distributed to customers. The link enables split payment processing where funds are automatically distributed according to recipient specifications.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate, but description lacks clarity on how split payments work and what the callback URL does. Added explanation of fund distribution mechanism and callback purpose to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'getPayInTransactions' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details. Original description has grammatical issues and lacks specificity about return format and pagination. Enhanced description clarifies that results are paginated, specifies the Payment schema is returned, and mentions filtering capabilities for better AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Fetches a  list of incoming payment transactions (pay-ins) for the authenticated user. Useful for monitoring received payments, notification and creation of downstream events
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of incoming payment transactions (pay-ins) for the authenticated user. Returns Payment objects containing transaction details including amount, status, timestamp, and payer information. Useful for monitoring received payments and triggering downstream event notifications. Supports filtering and pagination parameters.
data.reasoning: Original description has grammatical issues and lacks specificity about return format and pagination. Enhanced description clarifies that results are paginated, specifies the Payment schema is returned, and mentions filtering capabilities for better AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'getUnusedPaymentLinks' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details. Summary is clear, but description lacks specificity about what Link object properties are returned. Added details about return schema and clarified that 'unused' means unredeemed for better AI agent understanding of the operation's output.

data.current_description: Fetches a paginated list of unused payment links that were created by the authenticated user. Useful for managing and tracking pending payment requests.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of unused (unredeemed) payment links created by the authenticated user. Returns Link objects containing link details, creation date, expiration status, and associated amounts. Useful for managing and tracking pending payment requests that have not yet been completed by recipients.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear, but description lacks specificity about what Link object properties are returned. Added details about return schema and clarified that 'unused' means unredeemed for better AI agent understanding of the operation's output.

message: Operation 'getUsedPaymentLinks' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details. Summary is clear, but description lacks specificity about returned Link object properties and redemption details. Enhanced description clarifies what information is returned to help AI agents understand the operation's output and use cases.

data.current_description: Fetches a paginated list of used (redeemed) payment links that were created by the authenticated user. Useful for tracking completed payments.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of used (redeemed) payment links created by the authenticated user. Returns Link objects containing link details, redemption date, payment amount, and transaction status. Useful for tracking completed payments and reconciling payment link performance.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear, but description lacks specificity about returned Link object properties and redemption details. Enhanced description clarifies what information is returned to help AI agents understand the operation's output and use cases.

message: Operation 'deletePaymentLink' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary contains release status note that should be removed. Description lacks clarity on input requirements, return value, and important side effects (e.g., restrictions on deleting redeemed links). Enhanced version specifies input parameter, return confirmation, and critical business logic about link state restrictions for AI agent decision-making.

data.current_summary: (to be released) Delete a payment link
data.current_description: Deletes an existing payment link specified by the link ID. This action is irreversible and the link will no longer be usable.
data.description_suggestion: Deletes an existing payment link specified by the link ID. Requires the link ID as input. This action is irreversible - the link will no longer be usable and cannot be recovered. Returns a confirmation response. Note: Only unused links can be deleted; attempting to delete a redeemed link will result in an error.
data.reasoning: Summary contains release status note that should be removed. Description lacks clarity on input requirements, return value, and important side effects (e.g., restrictions on deleting redeemed links). Operation 'refundPayment' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. data.current_description: Initiates a refund for a specific payment transaction identified by pay_id.
data.description_suggestion: Initiates a refund for a specific payment transaction identified by pay_id. Requires the payment ID as input. Returns a Payment object reflecting the refunded transaction status. This operation reverses the original payment and updates the transaction state accordingly.
data.reasoning: The description lacks clarity on input requirements (pay_id parameter details), what is returned (Payment object structure), and side effects (transaction state changes). Added these details to help AI agents understand the complete operation flow and determine appropriate usage.

Description is too generic and doesn't specify what transaction type is supported, what the PDF contains, or the output format. Added context about transaction types, PDF content, and use cases to improve AI agent interpretability and help determine when this operation should be used.

data.current_description: Generates a PDF document for a specific transaction
data.description_suggestion: Generates a PDF document for a specific transaction. Accepts a transaction identifier as input and returns a PDF file containing transaction details. The PDF includes payment or payout information depending on the transaction type. Useful for record-keeping and sharing transaction documentation.
data.reasoning: Description is too generic and doesn't specify what transaction type is supported, what the PDF contains, or the output format. data.current_summary: Create a new split payment link
data.current_description:
Generates a new split payment link using the provided recipients, amount, recipient note, and callback URL.
This link can be shared with customers for split payment processing.
data.description_suggestion: Generates a new split payment link that distributes payments among multiple recipients. Accepts recipients list, total amount, recipient note, and optional callback URL for payment notifications. Returns a shareable split payment link that can be distributed to customers. The link enables split payment processing where funds are automatically distributed according to recipient specifications.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate, but description lacks clarity on how split payments work and what the callback URL does. Added explanation of fund distribution mechanism and callback purpose to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: Fetches a  list of incoming payment transactions (pay-ins) for the authenticated user. Useful for monitoring received payments, notification and creation of downstream events
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of incoming payment transactions (pay-ins) for the authenticated user. Returns Payment objects containing transaction details including amount, status, timestamp, and payer information. Useful for monitoring received payments and triggering downstream event notifications. Supports filtering and pagination parameters.
data.reasoning: Original description has grammatical issues and lacks specificity about return format and pagination. Enhanced description clarifies that results are paginated, specifies the Payment schema is returned, and mentions filtering capabilities for better AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Fetches a paginated list of unused payment links that were created by the authenticated user. Useful for managing and tracking pending payment requests.

Returns Link objects containing link details, creation date, expiration status, and associated amounts. Useful for managing and tracking pending payment requests that have not yet been completed by recipients.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear, but description lacks specificity about what Link object properties are returned. Added details about return schema and clarified that 'unused' means unredeemed for better AI agent understanding of the operation's output.

data.current_summary: (to be released) Delete a payment link
data.current_description: Deletes an existing payment link specified by the link ID. This action is irreversible and the link will no longer be usable.
data.description_suggestion: Deletes an existing payment link specified by the link ID. Requires the link ID as input. This action is irreversible - the link will no longer be usable and cannot be recovered. Returns a confirmation response. Note: Only unused links can be deleted; attempting to delete a redeemed link will result in an error.
data.reasoning: Summary contains release status note that should be removed.
